title: Iberis rifana
intro: Iberis amara, also known as Iberis rifana, is a species of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae). Iberis amara has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its potential to aid in digestion and relieve respiratory issues. The plant contains a range of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glucosinolates, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Iberis amara is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves, to take advantage of its healing properties.
taxonomy: Iberis rifana belongs to the family Brassicaceae. It is a species of flowering plant that is native to Morocco.
